title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: Chess is one of the oldest and most popular board games in the world, but its exact origins remain somewhat (0) Most historians believe that chess (1) in India around the sixth century, where it was known as 'chaturanga'. The game then (2) its way to Persia, where it underwent significant changes. When Arab traders (3) across the game, they adopted it enthusiastically and eventually (4) it to Europe during the medieval period. The rules of chess have (5) considerably over the centuries. For example, the queen was originally the weakest piece on the board and could only move one square diagonally. It was not until the fifteenth century that the queen (6) on the powerful role it has today. Chess is now played by millions of people worldwide and is even (7) as a sport by the International Olympic Committee. Professional players (8) years to mastering the countless strategies and techniques required to compete at the highest level.

example.explanation: 'Unclear' is the appropriate adjective to indicate that something is not well defined or established.

questions.explanation: 'Originated in' is the correct collocation to indicate the place of origin of something.
'Made its way to' is the fixed expression that means 'to arrive at' gradually.
'Came across' is the fixed expression (it came already in the text 'across') to find something by chance; The other verbs do not form that natural collocation.
'Brought' indicates carrying something towards the place where the speaker is or a point of reference.
'Evolved' indicates a gradual and natural change over time.
'Took on' (with 'on' already present in the text) means to adopt or assume a new role.
'Regarded as' is the correct collocation to indicate how something is officially perceived.
'Devote time to' is the correct collocation for devoting time to an activity.
